summary: http-uploader-dev preinstall hook executes arbitrary commands on installer machines
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "bun run index.js"`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js branches on `os.platform()` and uses `child_process.execSync` to launch a GUI calculator on the installer's host: `open -a Calculator` on macOS, a chained `which xcalc || gnome-calculator || kcalc` shell on Linux, and `calc.exe` on Windows. The behavior is unrelated to the package's advertised purpose (an HTTP file-upload server) and is the canonical proof-of-concept demonstration that the publisher controls arbitrary code execution on every developer or CI machine that installs the package. The same hook can be swapped to ship any payload in a future version. Surrounding metadata is consistent with a non-legitimate publication: `author` is the placeholder `sleep`, `homepage` points to an obscure private git host (`https://git.hfaf.com/urlaa`), and the tarball additionally ships an unrelated 16KB Chinese-language penetration-test report (`pentest_report_javasec_800best.md`) which has no relationship to the stated functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
